---
title: "Case Study"
subtitle: "Reconstruction of infection dynamics during a pandemic"
---

## Overview

Participants analyse simulated outbreak data from a pandemic occurring in a closed structured population of 1,000,000 individuals. The simulation uses an individual-based model (HERMES) with an SIRVD structure, incorporating household, school, workplace, and community transmission, as well as vaccination and disease progression.

Three datasets are available — incidence surveillance data, serological survey data, and contact tracing data — along with individual-level demographic information. Groups choose one of four questions (A–D) to focus on, drawing on whichever datasets are relevant.

::: {.callout-important}
The complete individual-level dataset is not revealed until the end of the workshop.
:::

---

## Case Study Document

```{=html}
<div class="doc-cards">
  <a href="assets/Case_study1.docx" class="doc-card" download>
    <img src="assets/thumbnails/Case_study1.png" alt="Case Study" onerror="this.src='assets/thumbnails/placeholder.svg'">
    <div class="doc-card-label">Case Study I</div>
  </a>
</div>
```

---

## Questions

### Group A — Key Epidemiological Parameters

Estimate the basic and effective reproduction number, recovery time, incubation period, and reporting delay distribution using the incidence and contact tracing data.

### Group B — Serial Interval Distribution

Derive the serial interval distribution from the contact tracing data, and use it to inform reproduction number estimation via the Euler-Lotka equation.

### Group C — Reporting Delays and Underreporting

Estimate the reporting-delay distribution and quantify the degree of underreporting in the available incidence data.

### Group D — Vaccine Effectiveness

Estimate vaccine effectiveness against infection over time using individual-level vaccination history and breakthrough infection data, and assess the role of susceptible depletion in producing apparent waning.

---

## Available Datasets

| Dataset | Contents |
|---------|----------|
| `final_pop_data` | Individual-level demographic data (age, sex, household, workplace, vaccination and death times) |
| `final_incidence_data` | Reported cases with symptom onset and reporting times |
| `final_sero_data` | Repeated cross-sectional serological survey at days 10, 20, and 30 |
| `final_contact_tracing_data` | Infector–infectee pairs with symptom onset times (days 10–20) |
